status: P1 EVIDENCE CLOSURE WAVE 8 / REFERENCE-SEQUENCE OR IDENTITY-CONFLICT CONTROL ACTIVE / NOT PLATINUM CERTIFIED
classification: Supplier-named pancreatic short regulatory peptide family
mechanism_summary: No canonical mechanism is assigned. The product name suggests an organ-targeted commercial positioning, but exact sequence, target and pharmacology require direct primary-source and analytical closure.
evidence_maturity: Regional short-peptide, cell, animal or limited human literature may exist under this name or related names. Evidence is not attributed to the catalogue product until chemical identity and source linkage are confirmed.
regulatory_headline: IDENTITY-SENSITIVE SUPPLIER PRODUCT FAMILY / NO UNIVERSAL APPROVAL OR ORGAN-TREATMENT CLAIM
certification_blockers: Molecular formula, mass and reference standard
Current product- and jurisdiction-specific regulatory status
closure_class: KEDW TETRAPEPTIDE / TERMINAL AMIDE AND SUPPLIER EQUIVALENCE
closure_state: PARTIAL CLOSURE — PANCRAGEN RESIDUE ORDER LYS-GLU-ASP-TRP (KEDW) AND NON-HUMAN/HUMAN REGIONAL LITERATURE IDENTIFIED; PA20 TERMINAL CHEMISTRY, SALT, ANALYTICAL IDENTITY, STERILITY AND CLINICAL EQUIVALENCE OPEN
authoritative_product_boundary: Primary and indexed literature identifies Pancragen as Lys-Glu-Asp-Trp, while PubMed substance indexing uses lysyl-glutamyl-aspartyl-tryptophanamide. The evidence includes aged-cell, non-human-primate and small regional human reports. It does not establish PA20 analytical identity, a universally validated diabetes medicine or an authorised product.
canonical_reference_sequence_description: Lys-Glu-Asp-Trp; terminal amidation requires exact-product confirmation
critical_closure_controls: KEDW residue order is literature identity, not PA20 proof
C-terminal amide status remains supplier-specific until directly verified
Monkey and regional clinical reports do not establish universal efficacy
No diabetes, glucose-control, pancreatic-restoration or anti-ageing claim
No dosing, injection or reconstitution protocol
